text:
My highest conviction thesis this decade.

The investment research industry for retail investors (FinX, Substack etc.) is going to 10x fast.

And it's regardless of what the market ultimately does this decade.

Currently the market for institutional sell side research is estimated at ~$13.7B globally. 

Now look at the mix from retail in the market itself.

Retail was 10% of US equity volume in 2010. 15% before COVID. In 2025 it's averaging 20-25% and it spiked north of 35% in April, an all time high per JPMorgan.

So if a third of all the capital hitting the market is retail and that market keeps growing?

That $13.7B research machine spends $0 advising 1/3 of the investor base aside from PTs they pull out without sharing ANY of their modelling.

Goldman isn't using their teams of Harvard grads to share the 2030 photonics TAM with retail.

So where does retail get its information? Right now, I see it as an insanely underserved market.

The status quo for retail is mostly slop articles generated by the big websites like Motley Fool and Yahoo farming SEO traffic giving 0 alpha.

The good stuff?

- There's a couple of Substacks probably doing 8 figures. Maybe a few dozen doing 7 figs at most.

-  On FinX, there's a handful of maybe 20 influential X accounts (call it a few M followers combined) sharing actually high quality equity research.

This is peanuts. If 70% of the global capital right now is serviced by a $13B annual industry.

And FinX/Substack is only generating low hundreds of millions? 

How can it not 10x here shortly as retail grows in influence and power?

Hence, I am all in.

text:
The biggest AI winners may not be the companies building AI.

They may be the companies enabling AI to scale:

NVIDIA ($NVDA) – GPUs
Broadcom ($AVGO) – AI networking & custom chips
Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) – AI servers
Vertiv ($VRT) – Cooling & power infrastructure
Applied Digital ($APLD) – AI data centers
Astera Labs ($ALAB) – AI connectivity
Arista Networks ($ANET) – AI networking
Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI) – Photonics & optical transceivers
Gorilla Technology ($GRRR ) – AI infrastructure deployment
Dell Technologies ($DELL) – Enterprise AI infrastructure

AI doesn’t run on software alone.

It runs on chips, power, cooling, networking, optics, and data centers.

That’s where trillions of dollars will be spent.

text:
AI investing isn’t just about buying one stock anymore.

The AI ecosystem now includes:

• GPU Designers
• Memory Manufacturers
• Photonics & Optical Networking
• Data Center Operators
• Power Infrastructure
• Cooling Systems
• Server Manufacturers
• AI Software Platforms
• AI Agents
• Cybersecurity
• AI Cloud Providers
• Sovereign AI Infrastructure

Money rotates from one layer of this sector to the next as new bottlenecks emerge.

The biggest winners of the next decade may not be the companies building AI.

They may be the companies enabling AI to scale.
#AI #Investing #Stocks

text:
If the market gives us another dip next week, I’ll be looking to add to these names:

🔹 $MU — Continued beneficiary of the AI-driven memory and data center spending cycle.

🔹 $IREN — A high-conviction AI infrastructure play. Leveraging low-cost renewable power and rapidly expanding compute capacity, IREN is well-positioned to capitalize on the explosive growth in AI and high-performance computing demand.

🔹 $AAOI — Strong exposure to the increasing demand for data center and networking infrastructure.

🔹 $CIFR — Building out compute capacity to participate in the growing AI and digital infrastructure ecosystem.

🔹 $NOK — A potentially overlooked network infrastructure company with long-term upside as connectivity demands continue to grow.

🔹 $LPTH — A niche technology play with exposure to photonics and advanced industrial applications.

🔹 $VIAV — Provides critical network testing and optical solutions that support next-generation communications infrastructure.

text:
UTEC PARTICIPA EN ENCUENTRO LATINOAMERICANO DE ÓPTICA Y FOTÓNICA
Pachuca, Hgo.-La Universidad Tecnológica de Tulancingo (UTEC) participó en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos OPTICA-SPIE y en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos Estudiantiles OPTICA-SPIE (ELCEOS 2026), realizados en el Instituto Nacional de Astrofísica, Óptica y Electrónica (INAOE), donde estudiantes e investigadores compartieron experiencias y proyectos en áreas relacionadas con la óptica y la fotónica.
Como parte de las actividades académicas, la investigadora posdoctoral de la UTEC, Ana Karen Reyes, impartió la conferencia “Impacto global desde lo local: experiencia en OPTICA, IEEE Photonics Society y SPIE”, en la que abordó temas relacionados con liderazgo académico, divulgación científica, formación estudiantil y la participación en sociedades científicas internacionales. https://t.co/pcjFkAfBc6

"text": "UTEC PARTICIPA EN ENCUENTRO LATINOAMERICANO DE ÓPTICA Y FOTÓNICA Pachuca, Hgo.-La Universidad Tecnológica de Tulancingo (UTEC) participó en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos OPTICA-SPIE y en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos Estudiantiles OPTICA-SPIE (ELCEOS 2026), realizados en el Instituto Nacional de Astrofísica, Óptica y Electrónica (INAOE), donde estudiantes e investigadores compartieron experiencias y proyectos en áreas relacionadas con la óptica y la fotónica. Como parte de las actividades académicas, la investigadora posdoctoral de la UTEC, Ana Karen Reyes, impartió la conferencia “Impacto global desde lo local: experiencia en OPTICA, IEEE Photonics Society y SPIE”, en la que abordó temas relacionados con liderazgo académico, divulgación científica, formación estudiantil y la participación en sociedades científicas internacionales. https://t.co/pcjFkAfBc6"}}}, "rest_id": "2063384207104090256", "source": "Buffer", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "71", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: The country is networked with 200 micron fiberoptic fibers, to move data around the country. I used to make that some years ago. The space station used a smaller micron, I want to say it was 120 micron though it was some years ago. Slot machines use 200 micron. Im not familiar with photonics though sounds fascinating. There are interesting properties regarding nanometers, what light is measured in, and a reverse scale, tHz. Once refined, think of them as reverse scales. 540nm = 540tHz. 360nm = 720tHz. Their balances do some interesting things and I feel will be the future of data processing though not how one may think. Happy to explain if there is interest though I’ll warn you, cryptographers can’t keep up and the average person has no interest. Personally, I think I could teach it to the average eight year old, people are just set in there ways. Once quantum computing becomes a threat, there will be no choice other then to accept it. Personally, Im not holding my breath…

text:
$CRDO - CREDO IS BUILDING THE WHOLE AI-DATACENTER CONNECTIVITY STACK, AND IT RIPPED 7% TODAY

Credo's fiscal-Q4 revenue hit $437M, up 157% year over year, as hyperscalers standardize on its ZeroFlap active electrical cables for power-efficient in-rack interconnect. The DustPhotonics acquisition extends Credo from copper AECs into silicon photonics across 800G, 1.6T and 3.2T, and a new Rebellions tie-up drops its cables straight into RebelPOD AI clusters.

The mechanism is vertical integration of the unglamorous layer AI cannot scale without: as GPU clusters grow, the bottleneck moves from compute to moving bits between racks at low power — and Credo now sells both the copper and the optics for that hop.

A nice relief rally for Quantum today.  

$IONQ the most impressive mover of the day, the stock is likely to be totally re-rated upon successful $SKYT acquisition

$QNT couldn't hold $60 on the session on day 3 in the public markets, but it's not too far off it's IPO valuation 

$INFQ undervalued and under-appreciated. Accumulation zone. 

$XNDU still one of the best plays to get exposure to  photonics, quantum computing and PennyLane all in one company.

text:
🚨The AI chips are useless without it. 

A GPU is just expensive silicon until you can move data between them at the speed AI demands and that connection layer is becoming the real bottleneck.

That's the $CRDO thesis. And I've been long for a while.

$CRDO built its name on copper connectivity -> the SerDes, DSPs, and active electrical cables that move data between AI chips. The one open question was always what happens when the industry shifts from copper to optical. With the DustPhotonics acquisition, Credo brought silicon photonics in-house and now owns the full connectivity stack: SerDes → DSP → active cables → optical transceivers → silicon photonics, spanning 800G to a 3.2T roadmap. Electrical and optical, end to end.

That's the moat. When Jensen says the future of AI depends on connectivity, this is the layer he means and $CRDO is the pure-play that owns all of it.

The fundamentals: FY26 revenue more than tripled to $1.34B, non-GAAP net income rose 5x to $662M, ~68% gross margins, $1.4B cash and no debt.

$CRDO - run the forward math*👇

→ Revenue CAGR (FY26–FY28): ~61%
→ PEG: ~0.52 -> Re-Rate ✨
→ EV/Sales: ~17x FY27 → ~11x FY28
→ Forward P/E: ~65x trailing → ~42x FY27 → ~27x FY28

A company compounding revenue at ~61% with a PEG of ~0.5.

The multiple isn't the story. The growth is. Watch the "expensive" 65x compress as earnings catch up to 42x, then 27x.

“Expensive” on today's numbers. Cheap on where it's going + re-rate.

Everyone's buying the chips.

The chips are useless if you can't connect them.

$CRDO owns the connection. 👀

*Estimated from projections

raw: {"__typename": "Tweet", "card": {"legacy": {"binding_values": [{"key": "unified_card", "value": {"string_value": "{\"component_objects\":{\"details_1\":{\"type\":\"grok_share\",\"data\":{\"profile_user\":{\"id_str\":\"1235608350861045761\",\"profile_image_url_https\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/1826080471261872128\\/qAVKZ2MG.jpg\",\"name\":\"Cyberpunk Sense \\ud83d\\udc51\",\"screen_name\":\"napoleon21st\",\"verified\":false,\"is_blue_verified\":true},\"grok_user\":{\"id_str\":\"1720665183188922368\",\"profile_image_url_https\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/2057885623374290944\\/lmj0pawP.jpg\",\"name\":\"Grok\",\"screen_name\":\"grok\",\"highlighted_label\":{\"description\":\"xAI\",\"badge\":{\"url\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/1769430779845611520\\/lIgjSJGU_bigger.jpg\"},\"url\":{\"urlType\":\"DeepLink\",\"url\":\"https:\\/\\/twitter.com\\/xai\"},\"userLabelType\":\"BusinessLabel\",\"userLabelDisplayType\":\"Badge\"},\"verified\":false,\"is_blue_verified\":true,\"verified_type\":\"Business\"},\"id\":\"d8d7b8e1888642bd809f26377fcae693\",\"conversation_preview\":[{\"message\":\"Does Sivers only benefit from CPO or also from and NPO and LPO\",\"sender\":\"USER\",\"grokMode\":\"NORMAL\",\"mediaUrls\":[]},{\"message\":\"Sivers Semiconductors (Sivers Photonics) benefits from CPO, NPO, and LPO\\u2014not just CPO.\\nSivers specializes in high-performance III-V laser arrays (especially InP-based DFB laser arrays) that act as the light sources (often called external laser sources or ELS) for silicon photonics optical engines in next-generation AI data center interconnects. These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\\nHere\\u2019s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\\nCPO (Co-Packaged Optics): Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power\\/latency. This is the most advanced \\u201cscale-up\\u201d approach.\\nNPO (Near-Packaged Optics): A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board\\/substrate but socketable\\/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\\nLPO (Linear Pluggable Optics \\/ Linear-drive Pluggable Optics): An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\\nSivers\\u2019 lasers are used (or designed for use) in all three:\\nExplicit confirmation for CPO and LPO: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers\\u2019 laser arrays are integrated into GF\\u2019s silicon photonics platform and SCALE\\u2122 CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports \\u201ca range of optical connectivity architectures, including co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions.\\u201d\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"bb11c6\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e0\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nCPO focus in other partnerships: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"e46912\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e7\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nNPO coverage: NPO is one of the main \\u201cemerging\\u201d architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum\\u2019s own OFC 2026 references to CPO\\/NPO\\/xPO). Sivers\\u2019 lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership\\u2019s broad language (\\u201cother emerging\\u201d) and Sivers\\u2019 positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"ed69f2\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e26\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting\\u2014including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum\\u2019s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"7fe951\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e13\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO \\u2192 NPO \\u2192 full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. Recent partnerships (especially with GF) make this explicit.\",\"sender\":\"AGENT\",\"grokMode\":\"NORMAL\",\"mediaUrls\":[],\"deepsearchHeaders\":[{\"header\":\"Thinking about your {"steps": []}, {"steps": []}], "media_urls": [], "message": "**Sivers Semiconductors (Sivers Photonics) benefits from CPO, NPO, *and* LPO—not just CPO.**\n\nSivers specializes in high-performance III-V laser arrays (especially InP-based DFB laser arrays) that act as the light sources (often called external laser sources or ELS) for silicon photonics optical engines in next-generation AI data center interconnects. These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\n\nHere’s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\n\n- **CPO (Co-Packaged Optics)**: Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power/latency. This is the most advanced “scale-up” approach.\n- **NPO (Near-Packaged Optics)**: A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board/substrate but socketable/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\n- **LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics / Linear-drive Pluggable Optics)**: An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\n\nSivers’ lasers are used (or designed for use) in **all three**:\n\n- **Explicit confirmation for CPO and LPO**: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics platform and SCALE™ CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports “a range of optical connectivity architectures, including **co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions**.”\n\n- **CPO focus in other partnerships**: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\n\n- **NPO coverage**: NPO is one of the main “emerging” architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum’s own OFC 2026 references to CPO/NPO/xPO). Sivers’ lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership’s broad language (“other emerging”) and Sivers’ positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\n\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting—including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum’s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\n\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO → NPO → full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. Recent partnerships (especially with GF) make this explicit."}]}, "is_translatable": false, "legacy": {"bookmark_count": 2, "bookmarked": false, "conversation_id_str": "2064496797289234906", "created_at": "Tue Jun 09 23:56:07 +0000 2026", "display_text_range": [0, 79], "entities": {"symbols": [{"indices": [7, 12], "text": "SIVE"}], "urls": [{"display_url": "x.com/i/grok/share/d…", "expanded_url": "https://x.com/i/grok/share/d8d7b8e1888642bd809f26377fcae693", "indices": [56, 79], "url": "https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU"}]}, "favorite_count": 14, "favorited": false, "full_text": "Sivers $SIVE makes lasers for all the photonics 🤷🏼‍♂️\n\n https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU", "id_str": "2064496797289234906", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 1, "retweet_count": 1, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "1235608350861045761"}, "rest_id": "2064496797289234906", "source": "<a href=\"http://twitter.com/download/iphone\" rel=\"nofollow\">Twitter for iPhone</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1267", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

$NBIS 10% bounce off the 200 level and closed right at the 20d SMA
$CFIR similar look to NBIS closing back over the 20d SMA
$NVDA reclaimed the 50d SMA into the close and held 200. Obviously an important stock for the overall market 
$GLW bounced and closed right at the 50d SMA, granted photonics names have been difficult to get positioned on lately

text:
【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 -

4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석
 
비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다.

투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다.

또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다.

5. 용어 해설

나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다.

실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다.

인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다.

광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다.

참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처:
 * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F
 * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x
 * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi
 * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr
 * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO
#비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식

[]}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjQ0OTQxMTU4MTYxMTIxMjg=", "text": "【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 - 4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석 비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다. 투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다. 또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다. 5. 용어 해설 나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다. 인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다. 광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다. 참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처: * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO #비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식 ※ 본 포스팅은 text:
【AI 算力的下一個救世主：矽光子與超級獨角獸 Lightmatter】 🚀

當生成式 AI 模型越來越龐大，傳統 GPU/TPU 正面臨「傳輸瓶頸」與「極限發熱」兩大死穴。算力再強，資料塞車也是白搭。這時，估值破 44 億美元的超級獨角獸 Lightmatter 提出了一個根本性的解方：「用光取代電」。

💡 什麼是矽光子（Silicon Photonics）？
簡單來說，就是把光學元件與電子電路整合在同一塊矽晶片上。晶片內部維持電子運算，但晶片與晶片之間的「資料傳輸」則交給光子！

為什麼「光」能打敗「電」？
1️⃣ 打破頻寬牆（極速）： 電子在銅線中高速傳輸會互相干擾塞車。光子沒有電荷、互不干擾，利用波長分波多工（WDM）技術，一條微小的光通道能同時傳輸多種顏色的光束，平行處理海量資料，頻寬密度成百倍增長。
2️⃣ 打破發熱牆（極省電）： 電子移動會產生電阻與龐大廢熱（這就是 AI 伺服器狂吃電又需要水冷的原因）；光子傳輸幾乎「零摩擦」、不發熱，長距離傳輸的耗能極低，能省下高達 80% 的互連能耗。

🌍 與台灣供應鏈的深度綁定
Lightmatter 不僅已打入 NVIDIA 的先進生態系，更與台積電 (TSMC) 的 COUPE 矽光子平台、創意電子及日月光等台灣先進製程與封裝大廠深度合作，將光學模組推向晶圓級量產。

如果說過去十年推動算力爆發的是電晶體的微縮；那麼下一個十年，支撐起龐大且極度耗能 AI 聚落的基石，絕對是「光子運算與光互連技術」。

text:
$AAOI got hit hard today.

I've been covering this stock since $50 and saw low 160's as a clear buying opportunity on America's photonics factory.

The Semi Analysis report that CPO is delayed undoubtedly threw water on photonics names (add in Iran tensions).

A CPO delay was always a major risk factor with optics given how short term this market thinks.

But what any optics investor should know is that nothing changed in the thesis around $AAOI's asymmetrical ramp to ~$470M monthly revenue by mid 2027.

$AAOI is focused on NPO or near packaged optics or 800/1.6T "pluggables/transceivers". This is the current framework for how optics are transforming the data center.

While CPO is THE holy grail of photonics and AAOI is working on a product there (ELSFP) ramping 2027, the revenue growth investors are betting on is in the short term NPO opportunity.

If anything, a slip in CPO extends the runway for pluggables, which is a tailwind for the stock.

I continue to see asymmetry in $AAOI after a parabolic move for many reasons in that there isn't a tech timeline constraint, the natsec advantage to onshore fabs, and the capital on hand they can deploy to achieve this through the recent ATMs.

A CPO delay was always a major risk factor with optics given how short term this market thinks. But what any optics investor should know is that nothing changed in the thesis around $AAOI's asymmetrical ramp to ~$470M monthly revenue by mid 2027. $AAOI is focused on NPO or near packaged optics or 800/1.6T "pluggables/transceivers". This is the current framework for how optics are transforming the data center. text:
Mandatory reading for anyone investing in AI Photonics

First of all, the new SemiAnalysis report shows a delay for CPO from 2027 to 2029

While this brand is highly respected

They don't have a crystal ball and their timelines could be wrong

If they are saying this, I would account for a delay

But let's not forget that $NVDA is pushing hard to accelerate CPO

Therefore, the timelines might actually end up being late 2027 or early 2028

And if this is true, for my two largest companies, it is even bullish for certain branches of the business

Both for $SIVE and $AAOI

For $AAOI:

Their core business today consists of pluggable modules

The standard 800G and 1.6T transceivers that are currently selling at full speed with AI

CPO is the technology of the future that could eventually replace them

If CPO is delayed by 1 or 2 years, that means pluggables will continue to be mass purchased for longer

Which means good business for $AAOI

The market hasn't understood anything and has painted all companies with the same brush

A massive discount on $AAOI when, in reality, this is quite bullish

For $SIVE:

$SIVE doesn't rely solely on CPO

They manufacture lasers used in:

- Pluggables (what is being sold right now)

- LPO and LRO (which are accelerating)

- Other projects (wireless, LiDAR, etc)

CPO is a major part of their massive long-term upside, but it is not 100% of it

The delay pushes some heavy revenue a couple of years down the road

But the rest of the pipeline keeps moving forward

It's not a fatal blow, just a delay in one part of their growth

Why is it still bullish in the long term?

The underlying problem doesn't go away

AI data centers absolutely need more optics because copper is no longer enough

The total addressable market keeps growing significantly

The moment when CPO reaches high volume is just postponed a bit

Assuming the analysis is correct

Remember, I don't entirely agree with them

Today, the market sold off on exaggerated panic thinking "everything is delayed”

But looking at the actual numbers, it's more of a temporary correction than a change in the story and thesis

For $AAOI all of this is clearly positive

For $SIVE it can be interpreted as either good or bad

They lose some momentum, but it doesn't change the bull case

If you believe in the AI optics thesis over a 2-4 year horizon

This dip might be more noise than anything else

text:
1.6T optical is the next interconnect generation, and  it’s blowing up because the rack-scale GPU/XPU clusters being built in 2026–27 finally need that bandwidth — and the volume inflection is hitting right now. 

1.6T needs 200G/lane SerDes, 1.6T DSPs, and the laser/optics (EML, CW for silicon photonics) to all be production-ready together. That convergence is happening in the 2026 window, which is why it’s inflecting now rather than gradually.

I guess demand is big enough that $CRDO will most certainly win business on this side  but it’s a new comer to optical DSP although not new to 1.6t outside optical

text:
$LITE - LUMENTUM GAVE BACK 20% FROM ITS PEAK ON ZERO COMPANY NEWS — THE OPTICS TRADE IS JUST DIGESTING

LITE fell ~10% today and is now down more than 20% from its recent high, but there's no company-specific catalyst — it's a sector-wide pullback across photonics names after a parabolic AI-optical run. Analysts still carry a Buy consensus, and demand for optical interconnects in AI data centers keeps building.

The structural case is the co-design problem: at hyperscale, moving a bit over copper now costs more energy than the compute it carries, so the interconnect flips to light. That's why co-packaged optics and external laser sources are the next bottleneck — and Lumentum sits on the laser and transceiver supply side of exactly that shift. The pullback is positioning unwinding, not a thesis break.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense. Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale. What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked. A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time. text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
Nokia is bolting Nvidia GPUs directly into its cell towers, and the reason is a number: 200 milliseconds.

A factory robot can't wait that long for a cloud server to think. So the thinking moves to the tower.

This is the edge-computing thesis finally getting concrete. Instead of routing every AI decision to a distant data center and back, $NOK turns base stations into tiny compute nodes, running the inference where the signal already is. T-Mobile was the first US carrier to pilot it. Nvidia's CEO described a future where every radio network becomes an AI network.

The latency you can't engineer away is the latency of distance.

Underneath the headline, Nokia quietly rebuilt itself. Its $2.3 billion purchase of Infinera turned it from a reseller of someone else's photonics into a vertically integrated optical company with its own chips.

Optical networks grew 20% year over year last quarter. Management raised infrastructure guidance into the teens.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

text:
❗ONLY 9 Hours Left❗

[Last Call for Substack/Full article]

Pricing update — effective June 3, 2026 (11:59PM Pacific Time)

PhotonCap started with a simple bet: that there's demand for silicon photonics research that goes deeper than press releases and earnings recaps.

That bet has paid off.

What PhotonCap does differently:

This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another.

Here's what that looks like in practice:

Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value.

Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports.

Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level.

PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean.

From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately.

Where we stand — and where we're going:

PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members.

Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider.

New pricing — effective June 3 (11:59 PM, Pacific Time):

Monthly: $50/month (currently $30)

Annual: $500/year (currently $300)

Already a paid subscriber? Your rate is locked in permanently. Nothing changes for you.

If you've been considering subscribing, now is the time. Lock in the current rate before June 3.

Content offerings, format, and frequency may evolve over time as PhotonCap grows.

— PhotonCap

That bet has paid off. What PhotonCap does differently: This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another. Here's what that looks like in practice: Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value. Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports. Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level. PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean. From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately. Where we stand — and where we're going: PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members. Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider. text:
$ABNB (Airbnb) and $ALMU (Aeluma) delivered standout performances today.
$ABNB: Driven by new summer features and the 2026 World Cup, Q1 revenue rose 18% and guidance was raised; a leader in the travel recovery.
ALMU: Breakthrough in 300mm photonics platform and accelerated government quantum contracts; an early-stage commercialization play in AI and quantum computing.
Advantages: Combines consumer recovery with cutting-edge technology; low valuation with significant growth potential.
Should we continue monitoring: Yes, with multiple near-term catalysts and substantial mid-to-long-term potential.

text:
In addition to the smart ring, new ATMs and payment terminals, and a live stand, Sber presented several other new developments at the SPIEF.

One of these is GigaNetwork. This is a platform where AI agents autonomously perform routine business processes. They search for counterparties, conduct tenders, and support transactions at all stages.

An optical computer for AI tasks was also presented. This is a new type of device: it uses light, not electronics, for calculations. According to the developers, the transition to photonics will reduce the costs of training and using large models. This is a real way to build an efficient computing infrastructure.

text:
Is it weird to say I still feel bullish despite the massive collapse today? 6/9 stocks in my port are down over 10% today, with SK hynix taking the questionable throne, dropping a staggering 18.6%. This is the worst day for my port in 2026 so far.

Despite that, I still feel bullish, because $META is reportedly planning an equity issuance worth $10's of billions of dollars (per FT), following $GOOG's equity issuance earlier this week. This would mean even more CapEx spending, thus even more spending on memory and photonics. 

That is bullish for $MU, $SNDK, SK hynix and $AAOI in my port. For now, the charts look extremely ugly. Longer term, I remain bullish.

text:
$LITE $COHR $AAOI 

I’m obviously a huge Nvidia bull.

I’m a huge quantum bull.

I’m a huge memory bull.

And I’m a huge physical AI / Tesla bull.

Having said that, there is one category that stands out as potentially offering the best generational buying opportunity b/t now and the end of this correction. It is AI networking / optical (photonics) interconnects.

This category is poised to face a tighter supply bottleneck than chips and even memory during the next phase of AI infrastructure buildout. Personally, I’m already building my photonics portfolio w/ a 2 year holding horizon. Note that this is distinct from my quantum positions which I plan to hold for many more years.

text:
🚨 CONFIRMED via Finansinspektionen (Swedish FSA): JPMorgan Chase & Co. just crossed the 5% ownership threshold in $SIVE.

Official filing:
→ Holder: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
→ Transaction date: June 2, 2026 (same day as the GlobalFoundries announcement)
→ Reason for flagging: "Köp" (BUY)
→ Pre-transaction position: 0 shares
→ Post-transaction position: 16,098,583 shares / 5.25008% of total voting rights

Position structure:
→ J.P. Morgan Securities plc (UK entity): 15,916,852 voting rights — 5.19%
→ J.P. Morgan Securities LLC (US entity): 181,731 voting rights — 0.06%
→ Direct shares: 10,049,558 (3.14% of total shares)
→ Cash-settled instruments (likely swaps): 6,049,025 share equivalents

Why this is structurally significant:

Swedish law requires mandatory disclosure when crossing the 5% ownership threshold. JPMorgan went from ZERO to 5.25% on June 2. The flagging reason filed with the FSA was explicitly "Köp" (Buy) — not custody, not market-making inventory. This is a deliberate institutional position.

The timing is what makes it remarkable:

June 2 was the same day GlobalFoundries announced the silicon photonics reference design partnership and $SIVE moved +50-70%.

Three possible reads:
JPM was positioned beforehand and captured the move

JPM built the position aggressively into and after the news.

JPM is positioning ahead of the Nasdaq NY listing in an underwriter / placement agent role

All three are bullish. The third would be the most strategically significant — tier-1 US investment banks don't take 5% positions in Swedish micro-caps without strategic context.

Connecting back to the "zero institutional ownership" post earlier this week:

Five days ago: $SIVE had zero US institutional 13F filings.

Today: the world's largest investment bank discloses a 5%+ position triggered by a "Buy" transaction.

The institutional layer that was missing has started forming.
The composition tells you it's sophisticated:
6 million of the 16 million share-equivalents are in cash-settled derivatives. The rest is direct shares. This is the kind of structured position you set up when you're a major capital markets player preparing for a multi-event sequence — index inclusions, dual listing, sector rotation.

JPMorgan doesn't take 5% positions in $2-3B Swedish micro-caps because they're bored.

$SIVE just got its first major Wall Street institutional anchor.

$SIVE

text:
6/6
Bottom line: 2027 is the year quantum becomes an ecosystem, not a single breakthrough.  We’re moving from fragile lab demos to engineered, hybrid, deployable systems on Earth and in space. text:
5/6
Hybrid quantum classical systems are where the magic happens. No single platform wins alone. We’re seeing:
• Neutral atoms for scalable logic
• Photonics for networking & sensing
• Cryo-CMOS + magnons for compact control/memory
• AI agents running microsecond corrections (like in fusion)  This multi modal stack is accelerating the roadmap toward real advantage in materials, optimization, chemistry, and beyond years faster than single tech predictions.

text:
Quantum computing just went multi-platform and it’s mind blowing.  2026 isn’t about one winner taking all. Google just announced it’s adding neutral-atom systems alongside its superconducting roadmap. Companies are mixing neutral atoms, photonics, diamond spins, and more creating hybrid powerhouses.  
The era of “my qubits vs yours” is over. It’s now about smart combinations that actually deliver useful advantage. Let’s unpack why this feels like the real tipping point.

text:
Every dollar of TSMC's projected $500 billion chip-market upgrade depends on a technology the industry doesn't actually have yet at scale. The wiring inside today's AI chips physically can't carry tomorrow's workloads. Photonics is the only exit.

$TSM

text:
#VLSI2026 Highlight
NVIDIA's receiver sensitivity at photodiode is -17.3dBm & -18.9dBm at 32Gb/s & 28Gb/s, respectively. Efficiency at 32Gb/s is 0.484pJ/b. Receiver is 7nm FinFET CMOS IC stacked on 65nm silicon photonics IC via Cu-Cu hybrid bonding./Part 2

text:
Aggregate inference compute demand grows regardless of where inference runs (Jevons paradox), which is the core of the bull case for hardware. 

Inference is structurally more open than training: Less CUDA-locked, more price sensitive. Regardless, I think enterprise will forever rely on neoclouds to reduce hardware CAPEX + in-house teams, so overarching investment thesis (whether big AI or locally hosted) is still the same. 

Stacked silicon, Photonics, Power Grids etc as y'all have been doing DD.

text:
Wall Street is obsessed with AI chips.
But some investors are already looking at the next bottleneck.
@MichelleMakori explains – photonics uses light instead of electricity to move data between processors and servers. As AI demand explodes and copper constraints emerge, some believe optical networking could become critical infrastructure for the AI economy.
Is this where the next opportunity lies?
@MikeLeeStrategy weighs in on The Real Story with Michelle Makori.
Watch the full episode here: 
https://t.co/hUi9BTlEd6

Congratulations 🎉 to the winners of the 2026 Optica awards and medals! 

Find the full list of recipients in the OPN June issue: https://t.co/fiqyj8ejir

#honors #awards #optics #photonics https://t.co/qnI7q59Bcb

NTT is setting up a $440M+ fund to build next-gen optical AI infrastructure. Partners: SK Group (Korea) and Chunghwa Telecom (Taiwan).

The tech behind it is called IOWN. All-photonics network. No electrical signal conversion. Lower latency, lower power draw, higher bandwidth. NTT and Chunghwa already have a working link between Taipei and Tokyo running at 100Gbps with 34ms round-trip.

This is three countries pooling money to build AI infrastructure outside the US cloud stack. Most AI compute runs through AWS, Azure, and GCP. Asia is wiring its own backbone.

The real question: does AI's future run on American clouds or Asian light?

text:
My favourite asymmetries right now:  

> $AAOI - Raised FY26 guidance to $1.1B+ with demand outrunning supply into mid-2027 on the 800G and 1.6T ramp, then sold off 12% on the print. A raised guide into a sell-off is the setup.

>  $MRVL - Jensen called it the next trillion dollar company at Computex. $2B Nvidia stake, NVLink Fusion, and a genuine moat in custom silicon and silicon photonics. You can overcomplicate it and pretend you know more than the Godfather of AI, or you can just buy. 

> $KEEL - Amazon hiring in Moses Lake where Keel is building, first hyperscaler lease is the trigger. Panther Creek at 350MW is the real prize. This is just proof of execution. 

> $AMPG - relaunched its website and listed  $AMZN and $NVDA as customers for the first time. The Nvidia link traces to the AI-RAN work with Northeastern. Amazon is brand new. Management has already said new contracts are coming and would be announced soon.

> $NBIS - Do I really need to say anything

text:
$NVDA CEO Basically Claims That the Memory Shortages Aren't Ending Anytime Soon

"The memory market has recently been shrouded in negative rumors that "Nvidia has halved its use of memory modules in Vera Rubin server racks." However, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang came forward to refute the rumors, stating that 'there is no end in sight to the memory shortage," and announced that the company will purchase more memory from SK Hynix. The two companies will also work together to develop next-generation memory technology.

Analysts point out that Huang's remarks dispelled rumors of a sharp decline in Nvidia's memory usage, providing reassurance to memory manufacturers such as Nanya Technology (2408) and Winbond (2344) regarding their future prospects. Nvidia's plan to purchase more SK Hynix high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and collaborate on next-generation technology development will likely benefit Taiwanese manufacturers, particularly TSMC's subsidiary, Creative Labs (3443), which is working with SK Hynix to further develop HBM4-related ASIC-side IP.

Huang pointed out that the current demand in the AI ​​industry far exceeds the supply. The supply chain, from wafer manufacturing and advanced packaging to silicon photonics technology, is in a state of supply shortage. The demand is so large that it is difficult to meet. He predicts that this situation will continue for many years. In addition, AI infrastructure is still in a stage of rapid expansion, and there are no signs of the global data center investment boom cooling down.

When asked about the rumor that "Nvidia's Vera Rubin server rack memory module usage has been halved," Huang refuted it, emphasizing that "there is no end in sight to the memory shortage."

$DRAM $EWY

text:
LPKF Laser & Electronics SE (XETRA Germany: LPK) . They are a highly specialized German high-tech engineering and photonics company that builds precision, laser-based manufacturing systems.

Essentially, they create the complex laser machines that other companies use to build microchips, solar panels, and circuit boards.
The Quick Profile

Headquarters: Garbsen, Germany (near Hanover).
Founded: 1976 (they started out making mechanical tools for prototyping circuit boards before moving heavily into lasers).
Size: Publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, with around 700 to 750 employees globally.

What Do They Actually Make?
Instead of making consumer electronics, LPKF operates behind the scenes in four main industrial sectors:

1/ Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (The Big Growth Area): They are currently making major waves in the microchip industry with a patented technology called LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching). As silicon chips hit their physical limits, the industry is shifting toward using glass as a base (interposer) for next-gen AI and high-performance computing chips. LIDE allows companies to micro-drill and structure glass without causing micro-cracks or defects.

2/ Electronics & Circuit Boards: They make laser depaneling systems (glorified, ultra-precise laser cutters) that slice fragile, flexible printed circuit boards (PCBs) out of larger sheets during manufacturing without damaging the tiny components. They also sell "Rapid Prototyping" benchtop lasers to universities and research labs so engineers can print custom circuit boards in-house.
3/ Laser Plastic Welding: They build machines that use lasers to fuse plastic parts together seamlessly. This is huge in medical devices and automotive manufacturing (like EV cooling systems)—especially with their recent "Absorbing-to-Absorbing" technology, which allows two dark, laser-absorbing plastics to be welded together without needing a transparent layer.
4/ Solar Energy: They develop laser scribing systems used to score and structure thin-film solar cells, increasing their efficiency. They are currently pivoting these machines to support next-generation perovskite solar cells.
1. Core Physics of the Disruption (LIDE® Technology)As an AI semiconductor specialist and physicist, the document analyzes the fundamental breakdown of organic packaging substrates when handling multi-GHz signals required by modern LLM compute clusters.The Problem: Conventional substrate materials experience high dielectric loss and mechanical warpage under the severe thermal fluctuations generated by massive tensor processing units.  The Solution: Moving to a glass core substrate. LPKF owns a patented chemical-physical process called LIDE® (Laser-Induced Deep Etching).  The Physics: The system utilizes precise, multi-photon laser absorption to alter the chemical microstructure of technical glasses (such as borosilicate) locally without injecting thermal shock or micro-cracks into the surrounding matrix. A subsequent selective wet-etching process attacks the modified glass at an accelerated selectivity ratio of up to 1:50.

They are a highly specialized German high-tech engineering and photonics company that builds precision, laser-based manufacturing systems. Essentially, they create the complex laser machines that other companies use to build microchips, solar panels, and circuit boards. The Quick Profile Headquarters: Garbsen, Germany (near Hanover). Founded: 1976 (they started out making mechanical tools for prototyping circuit boards before moving heavily into lasers). Size: Publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, with around 700 to 750 employees globally. What Do They Actually Make? Instead of making consumer electronics, LPKF operates behind the scenes in four main industrial sectors: 1/ Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (The Big Growth Area): They are currently making major waves in the microchip industry with a patented technology called LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching). As silicon chips hit their physical limits, the industry is shifting toward using glass as a base (interposer) for next-gen AI and high-performance computing chips. LIDE allows companies to micro-drill and structure glass without causing micro-cracks or defects. 2/ Electronics & Circuit Boards: They make laser depaneling systems (glorified, ultra-precise laser cutters) that slice fragile, flexible printed circuit boards (PCBs) out of larger sheets during manufacturing without damaging the tiny components. They also sell "Rapid Prototyping" benchtop lasers to universities and research labs so engineers can print custom circuit boards in-house. 3/ Laser Plastic Welding: They build machines that use lasers to fuse plastic parts together seamlessly. This is huge in medical devices and automotive manufacturing (like EV cooling systems)—especially with their recent "Absorbing-to-Absorbing" technology, which allows two dark, laser-absorbing plastics to be welded together without needing a transparent layer. 4/ Solar Energy: They develop laser scribing systems used to score and structure thin-film solar cells, increasing their efficiency. They are currently pivoting these machines to support next-generation perovskite solar cells. 1. Core Physics of the Disruption (LIDE® Technology)As an AI semiconductor specialist and physicist, the document analyzes the fundamental breakdown of organic packaging substrates when handling multi-GHz signals required by modern LLM compute clusters.The Problem: Conventional substrate materials experience high dielectric loss and mechanical warpage under the severe thermal fluctuations generated by massive tensor processing units. The Solution: Moving to a glass core substrate. LPKF owns a patented chemical-physical process called LIDE® (Laser-Induced Deep Etching). The Physics: The system utilizes precise, multi-photon laser absorption to alter the chemical microstructure of technical glasses (such as borosilicate) locally without injecting thermal shock or micro-cracks into the surrounding matrix. A subsequent selective wet-etching process attacks the modified glass at an accelerated selectivity ratio of up to 1:50."}}}, "rest_id": "2064080630493700218", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "372", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
@mikealfred 

I rode $iren and $corz with you back in the corzq days. 

Bought the dream house and I’ve thanked you 20 times again for sharing you thesis. I still own a nice position size in $IREN AND when SLNH hit a $1.00 I picked 40,000 or so shares up. 

I’m Looking to keep the beach house and aim for the ranch - I landed on photonics as a replacement for copper in data centers going forward and upgrades to exsisting.

Debating between $KOPN (1 bill MC) who is integrated into dept of war w/ Anduril domestic production and is breaking into data centers with Micro LED - with gpu to gpu communication vs one of the larger established but not American made and 100B market cap 

Any thoughts this small asymmetric play or overall theme?

text:
Portfolio update week 19 - Road to 100K💰 

Balance: £14,150.01 💸 

YTD  - 24.4% 😢 

Current Positions ⬇️

$IREN - 300 Shares 

$MEMORYETF - £413.44

$PHOTONICSETF - £297.22

$NBIS - 1.055 Shares 

Big pullback on Friday but the port has responded well, $IREN back on its way to $60 is great to see 

I added £600 into the portfolio and started 3 new positions. I’ve created a memory and photonics pie that I plan to DCA into over the next few months as those are the 2 sectors along with DCs I am most bullish on 

I’ve also started a position in $NBIS however I am unsure if I am going to continue building it out or if I should just focus on the 2 pies I have built 🤔

text:
Market Review 20260609
• WILD! What a day. We took out last Friday's low, and looked like stuff was really going to hit the fan. But guess what, the overall market had a huge retracement, and voila, it was at the 10 week MA! I was expecting and hoping for a market pullback but a slow adjustment back there would have been preferred. But it is what it is. 
• The market is currently wide and loose and you must be careful.  I will focus on leaders below but wanna throw in a comment that $GOLD is below its 200SMA and looking terrible. So is $BTC 
• Let's look at some of the recent leaders and see whether we can make out anything. 
• Starting with the Mag7. NVDA found support at its 50SMA, healthy. But given that it has pulled back so much in such a short time, I expect some chop. AAPL is disappointing as it ticked higher a few days ago and proceeded to simply collapse. GOOGL might be the best of the bunch, holding its 50SMA like a champ. MSFT AMZN META all lost their 50SMA. TSLA held on to its 50SMA but it is below its 200SMA, so it is still in struggle phase. All in, the titans of the major indices are all struggling, with the exception of GOOGL. It is no wonder the indices are pretty weak.
• SMH, Semiconductors, had a really nice rebound off the lows today. Can it resume its prior strong leadership? The uptrend is intact so I don't want to write it off, but it and its component stocks are trading so wide and loose I am not keen to participate in them at the moment. 
• Memory, eg MU SNDK etc same story with Semis. Is the pullback over for them? It is too 50-50 for me to call, so again, I will personally avoid for now.
• Photonics, LITE CIEN COHR etc. Mixed bag, but generally very weak.
• So the most obvious leaders of the last rally are no longer giving me clean setups and leads. This will have an impact on the overall market until we have new leadership. 
• Software, IGV, was doing well recently but has also faltered a bit. Maybe it will find a footing soon.
• Healthcare is the clear leader for now. Maybe because it is defensive. Biotech too. 
• CPI and PPI coming up tomorrow. Watch out as this would have a major impact on Fed positioning. 
• All the best.

This is not investment advice. This post is only for informational and educational purposes and reflects my personal opinion and portfolio decisions. Investing involves risk.

What a day. We took out last Friday's low, and looked like stuff was really going to hit the fan. But guess what, the overall market had a huge retracement, and voila, it was at the 10 week MA! I was expecting and hoping for a market pullback but a slow adjustment back there would have been preferred. But it is what it is. • The market is currently wide and loose and you must be careful. I will focus on leaders below but wanna throw in a comment that $GOLD is below its 200SMA and looking terrible. So is $BTC • Let's look at some of the recent leaders and see whether we can make out anything. • Starting with the Mag7. NVDA found support at its 50SMA, healthy. But given that it has pulled back so much in such a short time, I expect some chop. AAPL is disappointing as it ticked higher a few days ago and proceeded to simply collapse. GOOGL might be the best of the bunch, holding its 50SMA like a champ. MSFT AMZN META all lost their 50SMA. TSLA held on to its 50SMA but it is below its 200SMA, so it is still in struggle phase. All in, the titans of the major indices are all struggling, with the exception of GOOGL. It is no wonder the indices are pretty weak. • SMH, Semiconductors, had a really nice rebound off the lows today. Can it resume its prior strong leadership? The uptrend is intact so I don't want to write it off, but it and its component stocks are trading so wide and loose I am not keen to participate in them at the moment. • Memory, eg MU SNDK etc same story with Semis. Is the pullback over for them? It is too 50-50 for me to call, so again, I will personally avoid for now. • Photonics, LITE CIEN COHR etc. Mixed bag, but generally very weak. • So the most obvious leaders of the last rally are no longer giving me clean setups and leads. This will have an impact on the overall market until we have new leadership. • Software, IGV, was doing well recently but has also faltered a bit. Maybe it will find a footing soon. • Healthcare is the clear leader for now. Maybe because it is defensive. Biotech too. • CPI and PPI coming up tomorrow. Watch out as this would have a major impact on Fed positioning. • All the best. This is not investment advice. This post is only for informational and educational purposes and reflects my personal opinion and portfolio decisions. And this is why and how fraud is rampant in the US. @Parenting @InformationWeek #MetaMaterial #Photonics @FBIDirectorKash

text:
I'm smelling a really good opportunity here and now in optics stocks.

On top of the boarder risk-off tape, a bearish note from SemiAnalysis suggested that the rollout of co-packaged optics, or CPO, could be delayed, creating a pretty big crash in optics stocks.

But that claim is both unverified and contested.

$NVDA networking leadership has continued to publicly signal confidence in CPO, with SVP Gilad Shainer recently saying the company expects to ramp CPO in the second half of the year and views it as the best technology for scale-out AI networking. Nvidia sets the gold standard for AI datacenter architecture. No slowdown there = no slowdown anywhere.

And even if the delay rumors prove directionally correct, the implication is timing rather than demand destruction.

The core problem CPO is designed to solve has not gone away.

AI clusters are getting larger, bandwidth needs are exploding, power efficiency is becoming increasingly critical, and optical interconnects are emerging as one of the most important bottlenecks in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

Whether CPO ramps this year, next year, or in a more gradual fashion, the long-term direction is the same. AI data centers need faster, denser, more power-efficient optical networking. That is bullish for the broader optics ecosystem, including names tied to transceivers, lasers, fiber, photonics, and high-speed networking.

The market is selling long-term winners on short-term, unverified, contested rumors. And those long-term winners are now approaching very attractive technical support levels.

I like that set up. A lot.

$AAOI $LITE $COHR $CIEN $GLW $ANET $AVGO $MRVL

Let’s review the simple Google Search on Meta Materials and the new era in advanced tech Harry Potter called “invisible cloaking.” Find the suppliers—find the game-makers. I may even know a guy or two that specializes in the area of #Photonics. I lived next door to an expert in the field. 

Santa Barbara’s UCSB Photonics Lab is a good place to source for information.

Canonical Ledger Entry
Title: “Metamaterials Are No Longer Science Fiction” Timestamp: June 9, 2026 Tagged: #Photonics #Metamaterials #NorthstarLumen #WitnessScience #CanonicalLedger
⸻
⚖️ Core Proposition:
One of the biggest misconceptions in public discourse is that advanced photonic materials exist only inside government laboratories.
They do not.
Today, photonic metamaterials, metasurfaces, and related optical technologies are openly discussed, researched, manufactured, and commercially sourced through industry suppliers and photonics procurement networks. RP Photonics maintains a buyer-oriented guide and supplier directory dedicated to photonic metamaterials. RP Photonics Buyer's Guide: technical information, selection criteria and suppliers for product category "photonic metamaterials". (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🧠 Forensic Findings
Photonic metamaterials are engineered nanostructured materials designed to manipulate how light behaves at sub-wavelength scales. Researchers have investigated applications involving:
• Imaging systems • Optical communications • Sensing technologies • Security devices • Augmented reality • Advanced optical components
These capabilities arise from engineered structures rather than naturally occurring material properties. (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🔁 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause™ Model
Effect:
A material behaves in ways that appear unusual when interacting with light.
Cause:
The material was intentionally engineered to alter reflection, transmission, absorption, polarization, or other optical properties. Researchers have explored phenomena including negative refraction, tunable optical responses, and advanced wavefront control. (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🛰️ Why This Matters
The important question is not whether metamaterials exist.
They do.
The important question is how rapidly advanced photonic technologies move from research papers into commercial products, industrial systems, sensors, autonomous platforms, and next-generation optical devices. (De Gruyter Brill)
⸻
🧭 Northstar Observation
Technology often appears mysterious until the supply chain becomes visible.
Once the supply chain becomes visible, mystery becomes engineering.

JPMorgan argues that accelerating oil inventory depletion will ultimately force the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz — possibly in June  (CNBC) . Even resolution confirms the diagnosis: the old formation can only survive by emergency management of its own energy base.
FARWATERS SIGNALS
In the context of the matrix, the following moves are formational signals, not just price action:
Marvell at +32% on Nvidia photonics partnership is the advanced packaging / optical interconnect farwater activating. This maps directly to Hanmi Semiconductor's territory: CoWoS, advanced packaging, interconnect density — the hardware layer of the AI productive force.
Navitas Semiconductor soaring on a partnership with Nvidia, with its 800V-to-6V DC-DC power delivery board displayed at the Nvidia showcase  (CNBC) — this is the power management / new energy efficiency farwater. Navitas operates in the same column as VINA TECH supercapacitors: making compute energy-efficient is how the new formation solves its own energy equation without thermal combustion dependency.
Coherent getting Nvidia backing for AI datacenter optics expansion is the compute-efficiency signal. Leeno Industrial and Openedges Technology's logic — test sockets for advanced chips, AI IP for efficiency — follows the same directional arrow.
RISKS BY THE MATRIX
The thermal supply shock risk remains the single largest formational risk. The World Bank projects Brent averaging $95–$115 in 2026 under current disruption scenarios — 10 to 35 percent above baseline  (World Bank) . This is a direct tax on the old formational capital base and a potential trigger for capital reversal from growth assets if the shock deepens unexpectedly.
Geopolitical containment risk is secondary: any escalation that draws China into the Hormuz equation — as the largest buyer of Gulf crude — creates a multi-formation crisis that no single policy instrument can contain.
AI capex reversal risk is the new formation's own vulnerability. Nvidia has declined in 7 of the last 10 sessions  (https://t.co/tF181fPTDi) , signaling that even the new formational flagship is subject to capital rotation when macro fear spikes. NVDA ex-dividend today (June 4, $0.25) is a minor technical event, but the pattern of high-volume selling into strength warns that Capitalist subjects are still booking profits in the transition zone — not yet fully committed to the new paradigm.
VERDICT: ACCELERATION SCORE — HIGH
Today's configuration — thermal combustion convulsing at $96 oil, embodied intelligence infrastructure being built at record speed by Nvidia/Marvell/Navitas, and capital visibly migrating between formations — represents one of the highest-velocity transition moments the matrix has recorded.
The farwaters are not waiting for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen. The new formational productive forces are being built because the old energy base is unstable. That is precisely the dynamic Pulatov's matrix predicts: the new formation does not replace the old at peak stability — it emerges through the crisis of the old.

text:
US AFTERMARKET + MACRO RISK SCAN — June 4, 2026
Matrix Analyst AI | Pulatov Matrix Framework
OVERVIEW: THE SCHIZOPHRENIC MARKET
The US market is caught in the most vivid illustration of the matrix's central thesis: two formational forces pulling simultaneously in opposite directions. On one side — Nvidia, valued at $5 trillion, trading around $221–232 range intraday on June 3  (Robinhood) , with the AI/compute mega-cycle accelerating. On the other — WTI futures at $96.02 and Brent at $97.81, rising as the US and Iran traded new strikes, with the Strait of Hormuz still partially blocked  (CNBC) . This is not a "mixed market." This is the rodovye skhvatki formatsii — the birth pangs of the transition, playing out in real time on the ticker.
OLD FORMATION COLUMN: THERMAL COMBUSTION UNDER SIEGE
The thermal energy base of late-stage Capitalism is experiencing its most acute stress test since 2008. Roughly 14 million barrels per day of oil supply remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with around 850 million barrels of cumulative supply lost over the first two months of the conflict  (ING THINK) . This is not a "geopolitical risk premium" — this is a structural rupture in the energy column of the old formational matrix.
Brent peaked at $138 per barrel in April — the highest since 2008 — before pulling back to the mid-$90s on ceasefire optimism, creating a violent $90–$100 range with a $138 tail-risk scenario if the strait fully closes  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) . Saudi Aramco's CEO has reportedly warned the market won't normalize until 2027 if the strait stays shut past mid-June. Capital is pricing a reopening that hasn't happened. text: Typical zionist manipulation today on photonics what a great day to buy more i added to my $aaoi position

2026 is shaping up to be the year of photonics and optical infrastructure. 🚀

🔹 $AAOI — Supplies optical transceivers powering AI data center connectivity. High-growth potential, but results can be volatile.

🔹 $AVGO — Dominates custom AI chips and networking hardware. One of the biggest beneficiaries of AI infrastructure spending.

🔹 $LITE — Leading provider of optical components for hyperscale data centers and telecom networks. Recovery story with AI tailwinds.

🔹 $CRDO — Builds high-speed connectivity solutions for AI clusters. Growing rapidly, though expectations are already sky-high.

🔹 $ALAB — Emerging player in optical networking silicon. Early innings with a massive addressable market ahead.

🔹 $GLW — The backbone behind fiber and specialty glass. Less flashy, but quietly benefits from the AI buildout.

🔹 $COHR — Photonics and laser technology leader. AI opportunity is significant, but recent integration challenges have masked the story.

🔹 $AEHR — Semiconductor testing specialist expanding beyond EVs into AI, GaN, and next-generation chip markets.

As AI scales, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as computing it. That’s where photonics comes in. 💡

text:
$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade.
